metadata.title:
THE PRESS ARTICLES ON WAR ON


LABOR; LOCAL CONDITIONS IMPROVE

pages.raw_text:
min Spokane in the past year by
the Card and Label league, at the
head of which is Louis LIE Claire
In touching on the open shop fight
in the coast cities, Mr.. LIE Claire
said: II think thise movement is
dead in SO far as Spokane Is con.
cerned. The movers in the crusade
against union labor found the large
stores of this city handling big lines
of union made goods and feeling
kindly toward union labor. When
no support was foun din this quar-.
ter they gave up the effort and
quit. The Press articles are timely
and should be read by every mem
Der of organized labor"


Said Sam Evans, q member OF
the carpenters union: II don't be.
lieve our people have sufficiently
aroused themselves to the serious
ness Of this fight. While we are
moving along in apparent ease and
security, the other fellows may be
getting In their work in secret, and
the storm may thus break before we
realize it. However, l don't antici.
pate any trouble before the next
year, should there be any move
ment on foot. l think the articles
Of Mr.. Sleeth are fine and to the
point"


D. c. Coates Is of the opinion that
there was an effort made few
weeks ago to embroil Spokane en-
ployers into the fight Of Portland
and Los Angeles, but that not suffi-
cient encouragement was given.
Conditions between employer and
employee, with the exception of two
or three lines, are more satisfactory
here than on the coast, and l think
that Spokane was found to be poor
soil on which to sow the seed of
discord. However, it is well to be
and ready for the battle
should one come"
